channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: FULL LIST: University majors most likely to get you hired in 2026
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Discover the university majors most likely to get you hired in 2026, according to a new Forbes report. Learn which degrees offer the best job prospects.
Read More: https://punchng.com/full-list-university-majors-most-likely-to-get-you-hired-in-2026/

item.title: Zamfara won’t negotiate with bandits, says Lawal
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Zamfara State Governor Dauda Lawal declares his administration will not negotiate with bandits, vowing sustained military operations to end insecurity.

item.title: Ekiti 2026: PDP candidate Oluyede insists gov poll not free, fair
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
PDP candidate Wole Oluyede maintains the Ekiti election fell short of expectations, citing irregularities. He is consulting on next steps.
Read More: https://punchng.com/ekiti-2026-pdp-candidate-oluyede-insists-gov-poll-not-free-fair/

item.title: Leclerc wins chaotic British GP as Antonelli suffers late failure
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Charles Leclerc won a chaotic British Grand Prix for Ferrari as Kimi Antonelli's world championship lead was dramatically reduced after a technical issue left him outside the points.

item.title: Explained: Who will play at right-back after Spence blow?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Rob Dorsett says that Djed Spence will be unlikely to feature in England's tie against Mexico in the World Cup leaving Thomas Tuchel with more issues in the right back position and overall team selection.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Paraguay head coach, Gustavo Alvaro, has stated that he will not extend an apology to his French counterpart, Didier Deschamps, regarding the insults directed at the Frenchman from the Paraguay bench during their Round of Sixteen match on Saturday. The coach maintained that he did not hear any derog

item.title: 10 largest banks in Nigeria by branch Network in 2026
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: In an era of mobile apps and USSD banking, Nigeria’s largest lenders have not abandoned the branch. The country’s top commercial banks collectively operate thousands of locations nationwide, reflecting a deliberate strategy to serve a large, diverse, and still largely cash-reliant popula

item.title: Kazim Adeoti Celebrates Son Juwon Gentry’s Graduation With Heartfelt Message
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigerian filmmaker Kazim Adeoti is celebrating a proud fatherhood moment as his son, Juwon Gentry, marks his graduation. item.title: Where to preorder Grand Theft Auto VI
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Rockstar's long-awaited Grand Theft Auto VI is launching November 19th, 2026 for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series S/X consoles. The game will be available digitally at launch, with physical cases containing codes, not discs, so your options for preordering are straightforward. The preloading phase for

Manir Muhammad Dan’iya, has expressed grave concern over the deteriorating security situation in the state. item.title: Nigeria tackles IMF, denies spending ₦8tn outside appropriation
item.dc:creator: ICIR Nigeria
item.description: THE Federal Government has denied claims that it spent approximately two per cent of Nigeria’s Gross Domestic Product (GDP), amounting to over ₦8 trillion outside the 2026 approved budget. It described the reports as inaccurate and a misrepresentation of the International Monetary Fund’s

item.title: 'England just have to deal with it' | Mexico fans do all they can to disrupt Tuchel's team
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Michael Dawson shares his thoughts on Mexico fans setting off fireworks near the England team's hotel ahead of their World Cup match and says Thomas Tuchel's side need to focus on what they can control.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Chioma Obinna The Human Rights Writers Association of Nigeria (HURIWA) on Sunday called on the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) to clarify reports that it issued the Candidates’ Access Code for the 2027 general elections to a faction of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), warning

item.title: VIDEO: Why married men should not kill themselves to please women – Pastor Funke Adejumo
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: "To all married men, don't ever kill yourself trying to please a woman. I'm a woman speaking to you, and there is nobody who can please a woman to The post VIDEO: Why married men should not kill themselves to please women – Pastor Funke Adejumo appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Hero’s Welcome For Cape Verde After Gallant World Cup Defeat
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Despite the team's loss to Argentina, celebrations erupted in Praia in the early hours of Saturday morning, as fans commemorated the fairytale journey. The post Hero’s Welcome For Cape Verde After Gallant World Cup Defeat appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Jade Osiberu fires back at blunt “spec” conversations in marriages
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: She added that the issue is however publicly or privately telling a partner that they were never the preferred choice.

item.title: Woman found dead in church toilet as police launch probe
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Police in Cross River are investigating the death of a woman whose body was found inside a church toilet in Calabar. The post Woman found dead in church toilet as police launch probe appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Ballon d’Or organizers releases statement on possible winners
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The organizers of the Ballon d’Or award have said footballers who play anywhere in the world can win the prize. Their statement clears up a long-running debate over whether footballers must play for European clubs to stand a realistic chance of winning. With the race for this year’s Ball

item.title: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei absent from mass funeral for father
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s son and successor was not among the family members and thousands of mourners who turned out Sunday for a second day of ceremonies to honour Iran’s late supreme leader. Mojtaba Khamenei has remained out of the public eye since he was wounded in the strike that killed his fath

item.title: IGP Reaffirms Commitment To Science-Driven Policing In Enugu
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Inspector-General of Police, IGP Olatunji Rilwan Disu, has reaffirmed the Nigeria Police Force’s commitment to advancing intelligence-led, technology-driven and evidence-based policing during a working visit to Enugu State. The Force Public Relations Officer, CSP Anietie Okokon Edem Iniedu

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz has reopened — why that might be a problem for the oil market: Chart of the Day
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Transfer: Real Madrid confirm fourth signing under Mourinho
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Real Madrid have confirmed their fourth summer signing under their new manager, Jose Mourinho. The Los Blancos confirmed in a statement on Sunday that they have completed the signing of Denzel Dumfries from Inter Milan in a deal worth around £17.3m. The Netherlands international signed a four-year d

item.title: Solar rail could become common in Europe after successful trial in Switzerland
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://www.euronews.com/2026/07/05/italy-could-be-the-next-country-to-build-a-solar-railway-after-switzerlands-successful-tri Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48794704 Points: 9 # Comments: 3

item.title: Cape Verde celebrates team’s return after historic World Cup
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Cape Verde's football team is getting a hero's welcome on its return home after a historic World Cup run.

item.title: The AI brutality: Could the world do with a little inefficiency?, By ‘Tope Fasua
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: But the real question we should be asking is whether the world could and should actually retain some inefficiencies, rather than the relentless pursuit of efficiency and more efficiency that may tip the scale and lead us all into confusion. There will certainly not come any day in the future where h

item.title: Mexico fans fail in bid to wake up England players
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England players feel little to no impact of Mexico fans attempting to disturb their sleep by letting off fireworks outside their hotel.

item.title: Ondo: APC supporters Clash Over replacement of candidates
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The last is yet to be heard of the crisis rocking All Progressives Congress (APC) over the replacement of some candidates who won the primaries for the 2027 elections. Daily Trust reports that about six senatorial candidates and 19 House of Representatives ticket winners were replaced by the leaders

item.title: APM Demands Probe Of Alleged N8.8tn Off-Budget Spending, Seeks Full Disclosure
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Lagos State chapter of the Allied People’s Movement (APM) has called for a full-scale investigation into the alleged N8.8 trillion in off-budget expenditure reportedly identified in the recent International Monetary Fund (IMF) Article IV consultation on Nigeria, insisting that Nigerians de
